steps.title: Interpret the radical expression
steps.content:
The equation is $\sqrt[14]{(315n)^9} = (315n)^{24x}$. Rewrite the left side using the rule $\sqrt[k]{a^m} = a^{m/k}$:
$$\sqrt[14]{(315n)^9} = (315n)^{9/14}$$
Both sides now have the same base $(315n)$, so the exponents must be equal.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 24:
$$x = \frac{9}{14} \cdot \frac{1}{24} = \frac{9}{336} = \frac{3}{112}$$
The answer is 3/112.
